source_pr.title: lightning: remove the unnecessary `offset` field in the key encoding for duplicate detection
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Part of https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/29588.
Split from https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/30203.

Problem Summary:

The `rowID` that allocated by lightning is guaranteed to be unique, so we can remove the unnecessary `offset` field in the key encoding for duplicate detection.

### What is changed and how it works?

Remove the unnecessary `offset` field in the key encoding for duplicate detection.

I tested about 5 million rows for TPCC's  order_line schema.
```sql
CREATE TABLE `order_line` (
  `ol_o_id` int(11) NOT NULL,
  `ol_d_id` int(11) NOT NULL,
  `ol_w_id` int(11) NOT NULL,
  `ol_number` int(11) NOT NULL,
  `ol_i_id` int(11) NOT NULL,
  `ol_supply_w_id` int(11) DEFAULT NULL,
  `ol_delivery_d` datetime DEFAULT NULL,
  `ol_quantity` int(11) DEFAULT NULL,
  `ol_amount` decimal(6,2) DEFAULT NULL,
  `ol_dist_info` char(24) DEFAULT NULL,
  PRIMARY KEY (`ol_w_id`,`ol_d_id`,`ol_o_id`,`ol_number`) /*T![clustered_index] NONCLUSTERED */
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_bin;
```
|                                        | index-engine | data-engine |
| -------------------------------------- | ------------ | ----------- |
| `duplicate-resolution="none"`           | 31 M          | 194 M        |
| `duplicate-resolution="record"`(master)  | 71 M          | 238 M        |
| `duplicate-resolution="record"`(this PR) | 52 M          | 218 M        |

The results show that the write amplification of the index engine and data engine is significantly reduced.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: sort.Slice(dupPairs, func(i, j int) bool { (br/pkg/lightning/backend/local/iterator_test.go).

evidence: evidence: sort.Slice(dupPairs, func(i, j int) bool { (br/pkg/lightning/backend/local/iterator_test.go)
